MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant Cleaver-Brooks, Inc.S
Off Calendar Apr-13-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:

Jennifer McCormick, Esq. of FOLEY MANSFIELD (510.590.9500), appearing for moving party / Defendant Cleaver-Brooks Inc.,
Garen Nazarian, Esq. of BRAYTON PURCELL (415.898.1555), appearing for opposing parties / Plaintiffs Willi Schneider, et al.

Matter called, argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Defendant Cleaver-Brooks, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. However, if defendant did sustain its initial burden, plaintiff's deposition testimony and declaration create a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (gaskets, refractory, insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to plaintiff's deposition testimony and plaintiff's statement of undisputed material facts are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Court Reporter: Beth E. Grant, CSR #10943 of AikenWelch, Tel.(707)888-3689.
